explanation: Conversation topics have drifted from the stated objective
conversation_text: Hey, I've been reading up on this stuff lately. You know how people always talk about whether we really have free will or if everything's predetermined? I think my philosophy prof has it right – no free will at all. Whoa, really? Like, determinism? Come on, man, I feel like I choose what I eat for lunch every day. What's your prof say that makes it so cut and dry? Uh, yeah, he teaches straight from Spinoza, you know? Spinoza says everything follows necessarily from the nature of God or substance, like a chain reaction. No room for free choices. Spinoza? That's old school. But okay, what about quantum physics? Doesn't that throw randomness into the mix, so maybe we do have some freedom? Hmm, good point, but my prof always says quantum stuff doesn't help because randomness isn't free will either – it's just chaos, not choice. He cites Spinoza again: 'Men are mistaken when they think themselves free; this is due to their ignorance of the causes.' Something like that.
